title: Neuontobotrys frutescens
taxonomy: Neuontobotrys frutescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a succulent plant native to South Africa. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, order - Boraginales, family - Boraginaceae, genus - Neuontobotrys, species - N. frutescens. This classification shows the plant's position within the plant kingdom. The classification of Neuontobotrys frutescens provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

intro: Neuontobotrys frutescens, also known as Bitter Apple, is a shrub-like plant native to South America, belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Neuontobotrys frutescens contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities.
